{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nara, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Uguisuzuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/203833.html Nara city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/remain/uguisudukakohun/ Nara City tourist information home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nara, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Uguisuzuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/203833.html Nara city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/remain/uguisudukakohun/ Nara City tourist information home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nara, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nara, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nara, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Uguisuzuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/203833.html Nara city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/remain/uguisudukakohun/ Nara City tourist information home page]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11675843]] Wikidata name: Uguisuzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.69111111|N|135.85444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Uguisuzuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/203833.html Nara city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/remain/uguisudukakohun/ Nara City tourist information home page]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11675843]] Wikidata name: Uguisuzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.69111111|N|135.85444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Uguisuzuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/203833.html Nara city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/remain/uguisudukakohun/ Nara City tourist information home page]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11675843]] Wikidata name: Uguisuzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.69111111|N|135.85444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">

{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11675843]] Wikidata name: Uguisuzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.69111111|N|135.85444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11675843]] Wikidata name: Uguisuzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.69111111|N|135.85444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]] (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of [[:en:talc|talc]] have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[en:Uguisuzuka Kofun]][[ja:鶯塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11675843]] Wikidata name: Uguisuzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uguisuzuka Kofun | native_name =鶯塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Uguisuzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Uguisuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.69111111|N|135.85444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|27.3|N|135|51|15.25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa}} (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of {{ill|talc|en|talc|WD=Q134583|lt=talc}} have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}}, with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Uguisuzuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/203833.html Nara city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/remain/uguisudukakohun/ Nara City tourist information home page]{{in lang|ja}}

==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa}} (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south-southwest, and appears to be built in three tiers in the posterior circular part and two tiers in the anterior rectangular part. Its overall length is 103 meters, with a circular mound diameter of 61 meter, and rectangular front width of approximately 50 meters. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of {{ill|talc|en|talc|WD=Q134583|lt=talc}} have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.
A small square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}} tumulus and two small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}} tumuli remain in the front of the mound, which are thought to be ''baizuka'' secondary tombs. They are estimated to have been built around the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">
The tumulus is maintained and repaired as part of {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}}, with improvements such as laying grass, installing protective fences, signposts, and information boards. It is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Line|Kintetsu Nara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}

{{nihongo|'''Uguisuzuka Kofun'''|鶯塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugano neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139157|title=鶯塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== Uguisuzuka Kofun is a large tumulus built on the summit of {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa}} (342 meters above sea level) in the northeastern suburbs of Nara city, northern Nara Prefecture. The name "Uguisuzuka" comes from a legend that identifies this tumulus with the "Uguisu no Misasagi" in [[:en:Sei Shonagon|Sei Shonagon]]'s "{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. On the outside of the tumulus, thick ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones made of crushed stone can be seen all over the surface, and rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and figurative ''haniwa'' (house-shaped, boat-shaped, and lid-shaped ''haniwa'') have been discovered. In addition, an island-like facility can be seen in front of the front part. Surveys have been conducted so far, but no [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted. The burial facility is unclear, but a small {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a floral pattern and an axe-shaped stone object made of {{ill|talc|en|talc|WD=Q134583|lt=talc}} have been excavated from the mound, which are thought to be {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A stone monument was erected on the top of the rear mound in 1733, with the inscription "Uguisuzuka", by a monk of [[Tōdai-ji]], which owned the land that this tumulus was built on during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.
